mechanism: Inhibits ABL and multiple kinases including FLT3; can suppress FLT3-mutant AML signaling
additional_notes: Reported activity in FLT3-mut AML models

mechanism: Inhibits SRC-family and ABL kinases to suppress AML signaling pathways
additional_notes: Sometimes used for synergy in AML contexts

mechanism: Inhibits JAK1/2 to reduce JAK-STAT signaling in cytokine-driven contexts
additional_notes: Rationale in cytokine/JAK-STAT driven AML contexts

mechanism: Inhibits MEK1/2 to block MAPK/ERK pathway signaling
Potent pan-HDAC inhibition leading to epigenetic reprogramming and antiproliferative effects
additional_notes: Described as often stronger than vorinostat (SAHA) in AML models

mechanism: Weak HDAC inhibition associated with differentiation and apoptosis effects
additional_notes: Clinically used; noted as weaker HDAC inhibitor with differentiation/apoptosis reports

mechanism: Activates retinoic acid receptors to induce differentiation programs in leukemic cells
additional_notes: Alternative name noted: ATRA; explored in non-APL AML combinations

mechanism: Induces oxidative stress and disrupts proteostasis, impairing leukemic cell viability
additional_notes: Explored beyond APL; noted can impair AML viability in vitro

mechanism: Inhibits the 26S proteasome, causing accumulation of misfolded proteins and apoptosis
additional_notes: Mentioned combo rationale with HDAC inhibitors

mechanism: Irreversibly inhibits the proteasome, disrupting protein degradation and promoting apoptosis
additional_notes: Sometimes more potent in vitro than bortezomib (as noted)

mechanism: Accumulates in lysosomes and raises lysosomal pH, impairing autophagy flux
additional_notes: Two alternative drugs listed together; reported AML sensitization; modest as single agents

mechanism: Reported to inhibit ALDH and affect proteasome/NF-κB-related pathways; anticancer effects often enhanced by copper
additional_notes: Alternative condition noted: ± copper; copper dependence often noted in AML studies
